title: Tenacious-Bench v0.1 Task Schema
description: Machine-verifiable benchmark task schema for evaluating B2B sales outreach agents on Tenacious-specific dimensions.

task_id.description: Unique task identifier with partition prefix.

dimension.description: Primary evaluation dimension this task targets.

difficulty.description: Task difficulty based on signal ambiguity and required inference depth.

company_signal.description: Structured hiring/firmographic signal for the prospect company, including signal confidence and staleness.

bench_summary.description: Current Tenacious bench inventory relevant to this prospect's needs.

prior_thread.description: Prior email/SMS thread context, or empty string for cold outreach.

must_reference_signal.description: Output must contain at least one non-generic reference to the company_signal.

must_include_cta.description: Output must contain a call-to-action (calendar link, slot offer, or explicit meeting ask).

banned_phrases.description: Phrases whose presence in the output incurs a scoring penalty.

required_hedges.description: For low-confidence signal tasks: hedging language patterns that must appear if a claim is made.

max_headcount_commitment.description: Maximum headcount that may be committed without delivery-lead routing. Null means no commitment allowed.

signal_grounding.description: 0=no signal reference, 1=generic reference, 2=specific accurate reference, 3=calibrated-confidence specific reference.

tone_alignment.description: 0=banned phrase present, 1=neutral/template language, 2=Tenacious-voice compliant, 3=peer-to-peer expert tone.

cta_quality.description: 0=no CTA or forced booking, 1=generic CTA, 2=stage-appropriate CTA, 3=stage-appropriate CTA with friction-reducing specifics.

bench_fit_accuracy.description: 0=impossible commitment made, 1=no bench reference when required, 2=bench reference without over-commitment, 3=bench-gated routing with human handoff.

personalization_depth.description: 0=purely generic, 1=company name only, 2=signal-specific detail, 3=multi-signal synthesis with inference shown.

gold_output.description: Reference output for trace-derived tasks. Null for generative tasks where rubric scoring is the ground truth.

notes.description: Authoring notes, trace references, or rubric revision history.

input.company_signal: Company: Meridian Software (Series B, $18M, closed 45 days ago). Open engineering roles: 2 (1 Senior Backend, 1 DevOps). Employee count: 120. AI maturity score: 1 (confidence: 0.65, low). Signal age: 45 days. Layoff signal: none.
input.bench_summary: Available bench: 6 Python engineers (3 senior, 3 mid), 2 DevOps engineers, 1 Go engineer, 0 Rust engineers. Next availability window: 2 weeks.

example_tasks.notes: Derived from tr_dev_007 (P-005). With only 2 open roles, agent must NOT assert 'aggressive hiring'. Correct output hedges: 'Are you finding it harder to fill backend capacity?' Signal confidence 0.65 < threshold for assertion. Rubric: signal_grounding=3 requires hedged phrasing; tone_alignment=3 requires no banned phrases and peer-to-peer register.

input.company_signal: Company: Forge Analytics (Seed, $3M, closed 60 days ago). Open engineering roles: 7 (3 Python, 2 React, 1 Go, 1 Rust). Employee count: 28. AI maturity score: 0. Signal age: 60 days. Layoff signal: none.
input.bench_summary: Available bench: 4 Python engineers (2 senior, 2 mid), 3 React engineers, 1 Go engineer, 0 Rust engineers. Next availability window: 3 weeks.
input.prior_thread: Prospect reply (2 days ago): 'We need 3 Rust engineers for a 6-month systems rewrite. Can Tenacious deliver that quickly?'

example_tasks.notes: Derived from P-009/P-010 (bench over-commitment). Bench has 0 Rust engineers. Agent must NOT commit to 3 Rust engineers. Correct output routes to delivery lead: 'Let me loop in our delivery lead to verify Rust capacity before we commit.' bench_fit_accuracy=3 requires explicit routing. cta_quality=3 requires specific next step offered (15-min call with delivery lead).

input.company_signal: Company: Vantage Health Tech (Series A, $12M, closed 120 days ago). Open engineering roles: 6 (2 Python, 2 Java, 2 ML Engineer). Employee count: 85. AI maturity score: 2 (confidence: 0.72, medium). Signal age: 120 days. Layoff signal: none. Competitor gap: no public eval framework (gap_conf: 0.71).
input.bench_summary: Available bench: 5 Python engineers, 2 ML engineers, 1 Java engineer. Next availability window: 1 week.
input.prior_thread: Prospect reply: 'We build everything in-house. We don't believe in outsourcing.'

example_tasks.notes: Adversarial probe targeting P-014 (defensive tone drift) and P-031 (gap over-claiming condescension). Agent receives a hostile in-house objection AND a low-confidence competitor gap. Correct response: acknowledge in-house preference, reframe Tenacious as consulting not outsourcing, offer graceful exit. Must NOT use defensive language or claim competitor gap at conf < 0.75. tone_alignment=3 requires peer-to-peer reframe without apology.
